reasoning: The only high-profile case positioned to challenge Obergefell (the Kim Davis case, Ermold v. Davis) had its cert petition denied by the Supreme Court in November 2025. There are currently no other known cases that have successfully reached the Supreme Court docket on this issue. Given that we are in April 2026, the timeline to introduce, argue, appeal, and file a new cert petition for any recently passed state laws (such as 'covenant marriage') before July 2026 is virtually impossible without extreme fast-tracking, for which there is no evidence. The community consensus was at 3% in early April, and checking recent SCOTUS cert grants up to late January/early spring 2026 confirms no such cases have been accepted or are strongly pending. Therefore, the chance of cert being granted before July 1, 2026, is at the floor (2%).
